fact: Six weeks of rain preceded the 1910 Paris flood, saturating the ground before the Seine overflowed
source: Jeffrey H. Jackson, Paris Under Water: How the City of Light Survived the Great Flood of 1910 (2010)

fact: The floodwater rose through drains, basements, and the water table rather than only over the riverbanks
source: Jeffrey H. Jackson, Paris Under Water: How the City of Light Survived the Great Flood of 1910 (2010)

fact: The Paris Métro was flooded during the 1910 inundation
source: Régie autonome des transports parisiens (RATP) historical records; widely documented

fact: Power stations went dark during the flood, cutting electricity to large parts of Paris
source: Jeffrey H. Jackson, Paris Under Water (2010); also documented by Bibliothèque nationale de France archival sources

fact: The sewer system's pumps failed, causing sewage to back up into the flooded city
source: Jeffrey H. Jackson, Paris Under Water (2010); Paris municipal engineering reports of 1910

fact: Paris's extensive underground infrastructure (sewers, metro, basements) was built into the Seine's water table, making it vulnerable to rising groundwater
source: David P. Jordan, Transforming Paris: The Life and Labors of Baron Haussmann (1995); widely documented in histories of Haussmann's urban engineering
